title: centaurea peregrina
intro: Centaurea peregrina, also known as the vagabond cornflower, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin from wounds and infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Centaurea peregrina is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to be used as a topical treatment for various health issues, and it is also used in traditional herbal medicine as a supplement.
